<!--
Code change PR.

May include doc updates alongside the code (entity comments, design-doc updates, README/CONTRIBUTING wording that follows from the change). Pure documentation changes belong in the Documentation template.

There is no "Tests" section in this template on purpose. Coverage delta will be measured per PR by tooling (planned, not yet shipped); manual self-report would be redundant once it lands. If something about your tests is unusual or worth surfacing — a deliberate gap, a flake you couldn't fix, a new test pattern, anything a reviewer would want to know that the diff doesn't make obvious — call it out in "Notes for review" instead.

If this PR changes how the project thinks — adds a new pattern that contradicts an ADR, deviates from a stated principle, sets a new architectural precedent — open an issue using the "ADR or principle change" template first. Per CONTRIBUTING.md, the project does not bend principles in code PRs.

Opening this PR alongside an in-flight ADR/principle issue is fine, and is encouraged when the "why" of the proposed change is easier to show in code than to describe in prose. The PR will not merge until the corresponding ADR lands; until then, treat it as discussion material attached to the issue. Link the issue in the PR description so the connection is obvious.

Comment blocks are for you — keep, edit, or delete them as you go.
-->
